methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $25M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Robotics & Manufacturing (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 11 same-sector peers exist in Robotics & Manufacturing, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Persona AI is a seed-stage robotics and manufacturing company founded by Nic Radford and Jerry Pratt that has raised $25M to date. The robotics and manufacturing sector includes 9 peer companies (1 public, 8 private), though comparable transactions at the seed stage remain sparse. Notable private peers operating in adjacent spaces include 1X Technologies, Divergent, Exotec, Locus Robotics, and Bedrock Robotics.

Given the absence of sufficient same-stage or adjacent-stage comparable transactions in the robotics and manufacturing universe, peer-median valuation analysis was not applicable. The analysis therefore relies on stage-progression methodology, which applies a typical seed-to-next-stage step-up multiplier of 2.5x to the $25M raised proxy, implying a valuation of $62M. This method accounts for expected value creation between funding rounds while remaining grounded in observed progression patterns.

The triangulated valuation range is $53M to $72M, with a median point of $62M. This relatively narrow band reflects a single-method foundation rather than convergence across multiple approaches. Key uncertainties include the absence of recent seed-stage comparables in robotics and manufacturing, which limits the precision of both the step-up multiplier and the broader valuation framework. Refinement would benefit from additional transaction data at similar stages within the sector or acceptance of broader sector-level benchmarking.
